name: Jimmy Reed
abstract: Mathis James Reed (September 6, 1925 – August 29, 1976) was an American blues musician and songwriter. His particular style of electric blues was popular with a wide variety of audiences.

concerts.label: Blues at Carnegie: Muddy Waters, Jimmy Witherspoon, Jimmy Reed, Oscar Brown, Jr., Big Maybelle

works_created.t: Baby What You Want Me to Do

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: I went on the road with Jimmy Reed in 1957, all down in Florida
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Von Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: I was on the road with Jimmy Reed. Now you know, I’ve been out there with Arthur Prysock...
about_quotes.doc_title: Von Freeman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Stovall
about_quotes.evidence: I saw him once after that... So he came out and asked me, come on, have some dinner with him. I had some meatballs. But yeah, that's the last time I had son. Never met him anymore after that.
